title: Ifloga molluginoides

taxonomy: Ifloga molluginoides is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family, Asteraceae. It is native to Australia and is considered a weed in some areas. Ifloga molluginoides is a member of the subtribe Gnaphaliinae and the genus Ifloga. The plant is often found in dry, rocky areas and along roadsides. It is a perennial herb with small, yellow flowers that are clustered together in a dense inflorescence.

intro: Ifloga molluginoides, also known as false mullein or Ifloga, is a plant species belonging to the family Asteraceae. Traditionally, Ifloga molluginoides has been used in medicine to treat various health issues, including skin irritations, respiratory problems, and digestive disorders, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antispasmodic properties. The bioactive constituents of Ifloga molluginoides include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made from Ifloga molluginoides include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used in traditional folk medicine to treat a range of health conditions.
